Fairness Opinion Package
Trustee's Independent Financial Advisor Review
Company
{{companyName}}
Date
{{sentDate}}
Trustee
{{trusteeName}}
Financial Advisor
{{financialAdvisorName}}
§ 01Engagement Summary

The independent financial advisor was engaged by the ESOP trustee to render a fairness opinion in connection with the proposed acquisition of 100% of the outstanding stock of {{companyName}} by the {{companyName}} Employee Stock Ownership Plan.

{{trusteeName}}, Independent ESOP Trustee
{{engagementDate}}
{{opinionDate}}
Independence Confirmation: The financial advisor confirms that it has no material financial interest in the outcome of the transaction, has not provided investment banking services to the company within the past two years, and is independent of all parties to the transaction.
§ 02Transaction Description

The proposed transaction involves the acquisition by the {{companyName}} Employee Stock Ownership Plan of 100% of the outstanding equity interests in {{companyName}}, a {{state}}-based company operating in the {{industry}} industry.

{{companyName}} Employee Stock Ownership Plan
{{sellerName}}
{{purchasePrice}}
{{sbaLoanAmount}} — {{sbaLenderName}}
{{sellerNoteAmount}} at {{sellerNoteRate}} over {{sellerNoteTerm}}
{{closingDate}}
Transaction Structure: The acquisition is financed through a combination of SBA 7(a) senior debt and a subordinated seller note. The seller note is fully subordinated to the SBA loan and includes a conversion feature or repayment schedule consistent with ESOP debt service capacity.
§ 03Valuation Methodology

The financial advisor employed multiple valuation approaches to assess the fairness of the purchase price to ESOP participants. The analysis was based on the Quality of Earnings report, management financial projections, and comparable market data.

A.Income Approach. Discounted cash flow analysis using management projections for {{projectionYears}} years and a terminal growth rate of {{terminalGrowthRate}}. Discount rate applied: {{discountRate}}.
B.Market Approach — Guideline Companies. Analysis of {{comparableCompanyCount}} comparable publicly traded companies in the {{industry}} sector. Valuation multiples applied to normalized EBITDA and revenue.
C.Market Approach — Precedent Transactions. Review of {{precedentTransactionCount}} comparable acquisition transactions within the past {{precedentPeriod}}. Transaction multiples adjusted for size, growth, and profitability differences.
D.Asset Approach. Net asset value considered as a floor valuation, particularly relevant for tangible-asset-heavy operations. Adjusted book value of {{adjustedBookValue}}.
QofE Adjustments Reviewed: The financial advisor reviewed the independent Quality of Earnings report, which identified {{qofeAdjustmentCount}} material adjustments to reported EBITDA. Normalized EBITDA of {{normalizedEBITDA}} was used as the basis for valuation.
§ 04Fairness Conclusion

Based upon and subject to the assumptions, limitations, and qualifications set forth herein, it is the opinion of the financial advisor that, as of the date of this opinion, the consideration to be paid by the ESOP for 100% of the outstanding stock of {{companyName}} is fair, from a financial point of view, to the participants of the ESOP.

Conclusion Rationale: {{fairnessRationale}}
{{purchasePrice}}
{{impliedMultiple}}
{{fairValueLow}} — {{fairValueHigh}}
{{premiumDiscount}}
§ 05Key Assumptions

The fairness opinion is based on the following key assumptions, representations, and projections:

A.Financial Projections. Management's financial projections for fiscal years {{projectionStartYear}} through {{projectionEndYear}} were reviewed and relied upon. The advisor has not independently verified these projections.
B.Discount Rate. A weighted average cost of capital of {{discountRate}} was applied, reflecting the company's size, industry risk, capital structure, and projected growth profile.
C.Terminal Value. A terminal growth rate of {{terminalGrowthRate}} was assumed, consistent with long-term industry growth expectations and GDP growth assumptions.
D.Industry Outlook. The {{industry}} industry is expected to {{industryOutlook}} based on current market conditions and analyst forecasts.
E.Management Representations. The advisor relied upon representations from management regarding the accuracy and completeness of financial data, absence of undisclosed liabilities, and reasonableness of operating assumptions.
§ 06Limitations & Qualifications

The fairness opinion is subject to the following limitations and qualifications:

A.Scope Limitation. This opinion addresses only the fairness, from a financial point of view, of the consideration to be paid by the ESOP. It does not address the fairness of any other terms of the transaction, the underlying decision to establish the ESOP, or any tax, legal, or accounting consequences.
B.Reliance on Management Data. The financial advisor has relied upon the accuracy and completeness of financial statements, projections, and other information provided by management and third parties without independent verification.
C.Market Conditions. Valuation analysis reflects market conditions as of the opinion date. Material changes in economic conditions, industry dynamics, or company performance may affect the conclusions reached.
D.Not a Valuation or Solvency Opinion. This opinion does not constitute a valuation of the company or any of its securities, nor does it opine on the solvency of the company or the ESOP's ability to meet its debt obligations.
E.No Third-Party Reliance. This opinion is provided solely for the benefit of the trustee in connection with the proposed transaction and may not be relied upon by any other party without the prior written consent of the financial advisor.
§ 07Supporting Analysis

The following supporting analyses and exhibits are attached to or referenced in this opinion package:

A.Exhibit A — Guideline Company Analysis. Comparable public company trading multiples, selected companies, and implied valuation range.
B.Exhibit B — Discounted Cash Flow Analysis. Management projection assumptions, discount rate derivation, terminal value calculation, and present value of projected cash flows.
C.Exhibit C — Precedent Transaction Analysis. Selected comparable transactions, transaction multiples, and implied valuation range adjusted for company-specific factors.
D.Exhibit D — Quality of Earnings Summary. Normalized EBITDA reconciliation, material adjustments, and working capital analysis.
E.Exhibit E — Capital Structure Analysis. Sources and uses, debt service coverage ratios, sensitivity analysis on key assumptions, and repurchase obligation projections.
F.Exhibit F — ESOP Affordability Analysis. Projected debt repayment schedule, repurchase obligation funding, and stress scenarios demonstrating ESOP sustainability.
—Financial Advisor
